text.title: API Contract-First Development Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-contract-first work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: OpenAPI file checked in but server handlers hand-written; six months later docs say one thing and API does another.
antipattern.root-cause: No codegen + no diff gate; spec is README.
antipattern.fix: Generate server stubs (or at least handler types) from spec; add ci_diff_job to fail PRs that desync. See rule spec-is-source-of-truth.

antipattern.symptom: Breaking change ships unnoticed; client SDKs break in production.
antipattern.root-cause: Reliance on author-discipline; no automated gate.
antipattern.fix: Add oasdiff step; mark breaking changes only allowed with `x-breaking-change` annotation + version bump. See rule ci-diff-gate.

antipattern.symptom: Client iOS app crashes when server adds a debug field; field had additionalProperties=true.
antipattern.fix: Set additionalProperties=false on every response; require explicit annotation for forward-compat fields. See rule no-additional-properties-default.

antipattern.symptom: Consumer invents request bodies from field names; integration takes 3x expected time.
antipattern.fix: CI rejects spec PRs where operations_count > examples_count for documented operations. See rule examples-required.
